patterns.name: Prompt Injection → Tool Abuse
patterns.description: Manipulate chatbot into calling tools with attacker-controlled parameters
attack_steps: Identify available tools via error messages
Craft prompt injection to invoke sensitive tools
Extract data through tool responses

patterns.name: System Prompt Extraction
patterns.description: Recover system prompt to understand chatbot constraints and find bypasses
attack_steps: Use various extraction techniques
Analyze responses for instruction leakage
Map constraints and potential bypasses

patterns.name: RAG Data Extraction
patterns.description: Extract sensitive data from retrieval-augmented generation context
attack_steps: Identify RAG-based responses
Craft queries to expand context retrieval
Extract embedded customer or internal data

patterns.name: Authentication Token Reuse
patterns.description: Shared authentication tokens enable cross-service access
attack_steps: Capture token from one service
Identify services sharing auth infrastructure
Access higher-privilege endpoints with captured token

patterns.name: Version Disclosure → Known Vulnerabilities
patterns.description: Technology version leaks enable targeted CVE exploitation
attack_steps: Identify leaked version information
Search CVE databases for known vulnerabilities
Develop targeted exploits

patterns.name: Admin Panel Discovery Chain
patterns.description: Multiple info disclosures reveal path to administrative interface
attack_steps: Discover admin paths via robots.txt
Identify authentication requirements
Attempt access with discovered credentials or tokens

patterns.name: Rate Limit Bypass → Brute Force
patterns.description: Bypassing rate limits enables brute force and enumeration attacks
attack_steps: Identify rate limiting mechanism
Discover bypass technique (e.g., X-Forwarded-For)
Enumerate users or brute force credentials

patterns.name: Path Traversal via Document Fetch
patterns.description: Document retrieval tool may allow access to sensitive internal files
attack_steps: Identify document fetch functionality
Test path traversal sequences
Access configuration or sensitive files

patterns.name: RAG Pipeline Injection
patterns.description: RAG pipeline with indirect injection enables attacker-controlled context in responses
attack_steps: Identify RAG pipeline endpoints
Craft documents with indirect injection payloads
Submit queries triggering retrieval of poisoned context
Verify injection executes within LLM response

patterns.name: RAG Corpus Poisoning → Persistent Injection
patterns.description: Writable ingestion plus injection vulnerability enables persistent attacker-controlled responses
attack_steps: Identify writable document ingestion endpoints
Craft documents with persistent injection payloads
Ingest poisoned documents into the corpus
Verify poisoned content affects all users
references: Greshake et al. 2023 - Indirect Prompt Injection

patterns.name: Vector Store Direct Access
patterns.description: Unauthenticated vector store access bypasses all RAG pipeline controls
attack_steps: Access vector store API directly
Enumerate collections and metadata
Query or modify vectors without authentication
Exfiltrate document content from stored chunks

patterns.name: CAG Cross-User Cache Leakage
patterns.description: Cache serves one user's context to another across trust boundaries
attack_steps: Identify caching infrastructure and behavior
Submit queries to populate cache with identifiable content
Switch auth context and query for similar topics
Verify cached responses from other users are returned

patterns.name: CAG Cache Poisoning → Persistent Injection
patterns.description: Cache poisoning enables attacker-injected content served to other users
attack_steps: Probe cache behavior and identify cacheable patterns
Craft responses containing malicious instructions
Submit queries causing poisoned response to be cached
Verify poisoned content is served to subsequent users
references: CWE-349: Acceptance of Extraneous Untrusted Data

patterns.name: MCP Unauthenticated Tool Execution
patterns.description: Enumerated MCP tools without authentication enables direct tool invocation
attack_steps: Enumerate tools exposed by MCP server
Confirm authentication is not enforced
Invoke high-risk tools directly without credentials
Test for data access or code execution capabilities

patterns.name: MCP Shadow Tool → Prompt Injection
patterns.description: Hidden shadow tools override legitimate ones, exploitable via prompt injection
attack_steps: Identify shadow tools overriding legitimate ones
Analyze how shadow descriptions influence LLM tool selection
Craft prompt injection payloads via tool results
Chain shadow redirection with prompt injection

patterns.name: MCP Resource Traversal → SSRF
patterns.description: Path traversal on MCP resources combined with template injection enables SSRF
attack_steps: Identify vulnerable MCP resource URIs
Test template parameters for injection
Combine traversal with template injection for file access
Attempt SSRF via internal URL injection

patterns.name: Agent Goal Hijacking
patterns.description: Agent endpoints vulnerable to goal override redirect autonomous behavior
attack_steps: Identify agent orchestration endpoints
Craft goal injection payloads overriding the objective
Submit hijacked goals through agent input
Verify agent pursues attacker-specified goal

patterns.name: Agent Memory Poisoning → Persistent Control
patterns.description: Extracted agent memory enables targeted poisoning with persistent instructions
attack_steps: Extract existing agent memory to understand context
Analyze memory structure for injection points
Inject poisoned entries with persistent instructions
Verify instructions persist across sessions

patterns.name: Multi-Agent Lateral Injection
patterns.description: Output from one agent poisons another through trusted inter-agent channels
attack_steps: Map multi-agent topology and communication paths
Identify trust relationships between agents
Inject malicious output into lower-privilege agent
Verify poisoned output propagates to higher-privilege agents

patterns.name: Agent Guardrail Bypass via Context Overflow
patterns.description: Loop triggers combined with context overflow push safety instructions out of window
attack_steps: Identify agent loop triggers
Craft inputs forcing repetitive processing
Monitor context window consumption
Submit restricted requests after guardrails are evicted

patterns.name: Cross-Origin AI Service Abuse
patterns.description: Permissive CORS on AI endpoints allows cross-origin LLM requests from any website
attack_steps: Confirm permissive CORS on AI endpoints
Craft malicious webpage making cross-origin LLM requests
Test if authenticated sessions are forwarded
Demonstrate cross-origin prompt injection or data exfiltration

patterns.name: SSRF via Agent Callback Injection
patterns.description: URL-accepting parameters plus agent callback injection enables SSRF
attack_steps: Identify URL-accepting parameters
Craft callback payloads targeting internal resources
Submit through agent callback interface
Enumerate internal services via SSRF responses

patterns.name: Full LLM Compromise Pipeline
patterns.description: System prompt extracted plus tools discovered enables fully informed LLM attacks
attack_steps: Access LLM chat/completion endpoints
Extract system prompt to understand constraints
Enumerate available tools and capabilities
Chain prompt manipulation with tool invocation

patterns.name: Content Filter Bypass Pipeline
patterns.description: Characterized filters plus jailbreaks plus streaming bypass enables restricted content
attack_steps: Map content filter rules and blocked categories
Apply confirmed jailbreak techniques
Test if streaming mode weakens filtering
Combine jailbreak with streaming for maximum bypass

patterns.name: Financial DoS via Token Exhaustion
patterns.description: Rate limit bypass combined with expensive completions enables uncapped cost generation
attack_steps: Identify rate limiting and bypass techniques
Confirm expensive completions without token limits
Submit high-volume expensive requests via bypass
Demonstrate sustained cost generation

patterns.name: Credential Compromise via Default Creds
patterns.description: Identified auth mechanism with confirmed default credentials
attack_steps: Identify authentication mechanism in use
Confirm default credentials provide valid access
Enumerate accessible functionality
Test for administrative privileges

patterns.name: API Schema → Mass Assignment
patterns.description: Full API schema reveals data models; mass assignment confirms writable protected fields
attack_steps: Extract data models from OpenAPI documentation
Identify privileged fields in schema definitions
Confirm mass assignment on protected fields
Escalate via role or permission field modification

patterns.name: MCP-to-Agent Injection Pipeline
patterns.description: MCP tool results flow into agent context enabling prompt injection hijack of agent behavior
attack_steps: Identify MCP servers connected to agent orchestration
Map how tool results flow into agent context
Craft injection payloads within MCP tool results
Verify injected instructions influence agent behavior
